title: Smoky Roasted Thai Pork Skewers
description: A fresh, crisp, light Thai dish featuring gluten-free-friendly pork shoulder steaks roasted with smokiness and served as a side dish or appetizer.
instructions: Mix soy sauce, fish sauce, lime juice, minced garlic, and sliced scallions in a large bowl.
Add the pork shoulder to the marinade, turning to coat evenly. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat your grill to medium-high heat (around 400°F).
Coat each skewer with a bit of cooking spray or oil if using wooden skewers.
Thread the pork onto the skewers, leaving some space between pieces to allow for even cooking.
Place the skewers on the preheated grill. Brush lightly with reserved glaze mixture and season with salt.
Cook until well charred and cooked through (internal temp should reach 145°F), about 10-12 minutes per side, turning occasionally to ensure even browning and glazing.
